description: Super admin login redirects to admin dashboard
steps: Step 1: Navigate to /session/new
Step 2: Enter super admin email and password
Step 3: Submit the login form
Step 4: Verify redirect to /admin
Step 5: Verify admin dashboard page is displayed

description: Non-admin user cannot access /admin area
steps: Step 1: Log in as a non-admin user
Step 2: Navigate to /admin
Step 3: Verify user is redirected away from /admin
Step 4: Verify a flash notice is displayed indicating access denied

description: User can log in and log out
steps: Step 1: Navigate to /session/new
Step 2: Enter valid email and password
Step 3: Submit login form
Step 4: Verify user is logged in and redirected
Step 5: Click logout / DELETE /session
Step 6: Verify session is destroyed and user is redirected to login page

description: Password reset flow via emailed token
steps: Step 1: Navigate to /passwords/new
Step 2: Enter a valid user email address
Step 3: Submit the form
Step 4: Verify a password reset email is sent
Step 5: Navigate to /passwords/:token/edit with the reset token
Step 6: Enter a new password and confirmation
Step 7: Submit the form
Step 8: Verify password is updated and user can log in with new password

description: Login rate limiting blocks excessive attempts
steps: Step 1: Navigate to /session/new
Step 2: Submit 11 invalid login attempts rapidly from the same IP
Step 3: Verify that requests are throttled after 10 attempts per 3 minutes

description: Super admin can view list of all client accounts
steps: Step 1: Log in as super admin
Step 2: Navigate to /admin/clients
Step 3: Verify page displays a list of client accounts with name, creation date, and trainer count

description: Super admin can create a new client account with initial admin user
steps: Step 1: Log in as super admin
Step 2: Navigate to /admin/clients/new
Step 3: Fill in client account name
Step 4: Fill in initial account admin email and password
Step 5: Submit the form
Step 6: Verify the client account is created
Step 7: Verify the initial account admin user is created and associated with the client
Step 8: Verify redirect to client accounts list with success flash

description: Super admin can view client account details with user list
steps: Step 1: Log in as super admin
Step 2: Navigate to /admin/clients/:id for an existing client account
Step 3: Verify page shows client account name and creation date
Step 4: Verify page lists all trainers and account admins (read-only)
Step 5: Verify user details include name, email, and status

description: Super admin can delete a client account with cascade
steps: Step 1: Log in as super admin
Step 2: Create a client account with an admin, a trainer, a master training, and a session
Step 3: Navigate to /admin/clients
Step 4: Delete the client account
Step 5: Verify the client account is removed from the list
Step 6: Verify all associated users are deleted
Step 7: Verify all associated trainings and sessions are deleted

description: Account admin can edit organization name
steps: Step 1: Log in as account admin
Step 2: Navigate to /account/edit
Step 3: Change the organization name
Step 4: Submit the form
Step 5: Verify the organization name is updated
Step 6: Verify a success flash message is displayed

description: Account admin can view trainer roster with status
steps: Step 1: Log in as account admin
Step 2: Navigate to /account/trainers
Step 3: Verify the page lists all trainers for the account
Step 4: Verify each trainer shows name, email, and status (active/inactive/pending)

description: Account admin can invite a new trainer via email
steps: Step 1: Log in as account admin
Step 2: Navigate to /account/trainers/new
Step 3: Fill in trainer first name, last name, and email
Step 4: Submit the form
Step 5: Verify user is created with status pending_password_change
Step 6: Verify an invitation email is sent via Mailgun
Step 7: Verify the email contains a signed invite token link valid for 72 hours

description: Invited trainer can set password via invite link and log in
steps: Step 1: Account admin invites a trainer
Step 2: Trainer clicks the invite link from the email
Step 3: Trainer sets a new password on the password reset form
Step 4: Verify trainer status changes from pending_password_change to active
Step 5: Trainer logs in with new password
Step 6: Verify trainer is not redirected to change-password page

description: Forced password change on first login for invited trainers
steps: Step 1: Create a trainer user with status pending_password_change
Step 2: Log in as the trainer
Step 3: Verify redirect to /account/password/edit (change-password page)
Step 4: Verify the trainer cannot navigate to any other page
Step 5: Enter and confirm a new password
Step 6: Submit the form
Step 7: Verify status is updated to active
Step 8: Verify trainer can now access the application normally

description: Account admin can deactivate and reactivate a trainer
steps: Step 1: Log in as account admin
Step 2: Navigate to /account/trainers
Step 3: Deactivate an active trainer via PATCH
Step 4: Verify trainer status changes to inactive
Step 5: Verify deactivated trainer cannot log in
Step 6: Reactivate the trainer
Step 7: Verify trainer status changes back to active
Step 8: Verify reactivated trainer can log in again

description: Account admin can permanently remove a trainer
steps: Step 1: Log in as account admin
Step 2: Navigate to /account/trainers
Step 3: Click remove on a trainer
Step 4: Verify a confirmation modal is displayed
Step 5: Confirm the deletion
Step 6: Verify the trainer is permanently deleted
Step 7: Verify the trainer no longer appears in the roster

description: Account admin cannot access another client account's data
steps: Step 1: Create two client accounts, each with an admin
Step 2: Log in as admin of account A
Step 3: Attempt to access account B's trainer roster or settings
Step 4: Verify access is denied or data is not visible

description: Trainer can view dashboard listing all master trainings for their account
steps: Step 1: Log in as a trainer
Step 2: Navigate to /master_trainings
Step 3: Verify the page shows all master trainings for the trainer's account
Step 4: Verify each training shows title, description, session count, and timestamps

description: Trainer can create a new master training
steps: Step 1: Log in as a trainer
Step 2: Navigate to /master_trainings/new
Step 3: Enter a title and optional description
Step 4: Submit the form
Step 5: Verify the master training is created and appears in the dashboard

description: Trainer can edit master training metadata
steps: Step 1: Log in as a trainer
Step 2: Navigate to /master_trainings/:id/edit
Step 3: Change the title and description
Step 4: Submit the form
Step 5: Verify changes are saved and displayed

description: Trainer can delete a master training with no sessions
steps: Step 1: Log in as a trainer
Step 2: Create a master training with no sessions
Step 3: Navigate to the master training
Step 4: Delete the master training
Step 5: Verify the training is removed from the dashboard

description: Trainer cannot delete a master training that has sessions
steps: Step 1: Log in as a trainer
Step 2: Create a master training and generate a session from it
Step 3: Attempt to delete the master training
Step 4: Verify deletion is blocked with a warning message

description: Trainer can upload slide files to a master training
steps: Step 1: Log in as a trainer
Step 2: Navigate to a master training's detail page
Step 3: Upload a PDF slide file via direct upload
Step 4: Verify the slide appears in the slides list with display name, upload date, file size
Step 5: Verify the download button works and generates a signed URL

description: Trainer can reorder slides via drag-and-drop
steps: Step 1: Log in as a trainer
Step 2: Upload three slide files to a master training
Step 3: Drag slide 3 to position 1
Step 4: Verify the new order is persisted via PATCH request
Step 5: Refresh the page and verify order is maintained

description: Trainer can delete individual slide files
steps: Step 1: Log in as a trainer
Step 2: Navigate to a master training with uploaded slides
Step 3: Delete one slide file
Step 4: Verify the slide is removed from the list
Step 5: Verify other slides remain unaffected

description: Trainer can upload prerequisite assets of any file type
steps: Step 1: Log in as a trainer
Step 2: Navigate to a master training's detail page
Step 3: Upload a prerequisite asset (PDF, video, ZIP, etc.)
Step 4: Verify the asset appears with display name, optional description, upload date, file size
Step 5: Verify the download button generates a signed GCS URL

description: Trainer can reorder prerequisite assets via drag-and-drop
steps: Step 1: Log in as a trainer
Step 2: Upload three prerequisite assets to a master training
Step 3: Drag asset 3 to position 1
Step 4: Verify the new order is persisted
Step 5: Refresh the page and verify order is maintained

description: Trainer can delete individual prerequisite assets
steps: Step 1: Log in as a trainer
Step 2: Navigate to a master training with uploaded assets
Step 3: Delete one prerequisite asset
Step 4: Verify the asset is removed from the list

description: Trainer can add exercises with rich text content via Trix editor
steps: Step 1: Log in as a trainer
Step 2: Navigate to a master training's detail page
Step 3: Click to add a new exercise
Step 4: Enter an exercise title
Step 5: Use the Trix editor to add rich HTML content (bold, italic, lists, links)
Step 6: Submit the form
Step 7: Verify the exercise is created with the formatted content preserved

description: Trainer can edit exercises
steps: Step 1: Log in as a trainer
Step 2: Navigate to a master training with an existing exercise
Step 3: Click edit on the exercise
Step 4: Modify the title and body content
Step 5: Submit the form
Step 6: Verify changes are saved and displayed

description: Trainer can reorder exercises via drag-and-drop
steps: Step 1: Log in as a trainer
Step 2: Add three exercises to a master training
Step 3: Drag exercise 3 to position 1
Step 4: Verify the new order is persisted
Step 5: Refresh the page and verify order is maintained

description: Trainer can delete exercises
steps: Step 1: Log in as a trainer
Step 2: Navigate to a master training with exercises
Step 3: Delete an exercise
Step 4: Verify the exercise is removed from the list

description: Trainer can preview exercise content as it will appear to participants
steps: Step 1: Log in as a trainer
Step 2: Navigate to a master training with exercises
Step 3: Click preview on an exercise
Step 4: Verify a Turbo Frame panel renders the exercise content as styled HTML

description: Generating a training session creates an immutable version snapshot
steps: Step 1: Log in as a trainer
Step 2: Create a master training with slides, prerequisite assets, and exercises
Step 3: Click 'Generate Session' on the master training
Step 4: Verify a version snapshot is created with version_number incremented
Step 5: Verify slide_snapshots are created matching current slides
Step 6: Verify prerequisite_asset_snapshots are created matching current assets
Step 7: Verify exercise_snapshots are created with rendered HTML body
Step 8: Verify a training_session record is created linked to the version
Step 9: Verify session name is auto-generated as '{Title} – Session {N}'
Step 10: Verify redirect to session edit page with success flash

description: Editing master training after session generation does not alter existing sessions
steps: Step 1: Log in as a trainer
Step 2: Create a master training with title 'Original Title' and an exercise
Step 3: Generate a training session
Step 4: Edit the master training title to 'Updated Title' and modify the exercise
Step 5: Verify the existing session's snapshot still shows 'Original Title'
Step 6: Verify the existing session's exercise snapshot is unchanged
Step 7: Generate a second session
Step 8: Verify the second session's snapshot shows 'Updated Title'

description: Deleting a slide from master training does not corrupt existing session snapshots
steps: Step 1: Log in as a trainer
Step 2: Create a master training with two slides
Step 3: Generate a training session
Step 4: Delete one slide from the master training
Step 5: Verify the session's slide snapshots still reference both original slides
Step 6: Verify the snapshot slide download still works via the cloud key

description: Trainer can view version history of a master training
steps: Step 1: Log in as a trainer
Step 2: Create a master training and generate two sessions
Step 3: Navigate to /master_trainings/:id/versions
Step 4: Verify the page shows a read-only list of all versions
Step 5: Verify each version shows timestamp and linked session count

description: Trainer can view session list for a master training
steps: Step 1: Log in as a trainer
Step 2: Navigate to /master_trainings/:id/training_sessions
Step 3: Verify the page lists all sessions for the master training
Step 4: Verify each session shows name, generated date, version number, password status, and share link

description: Trainer can rename a training session
steps: Step 1: Log in as a trainer
Step 2: Generate a training session
Step 3: Navigate to /training_sessions/:id/edit
Step 4: Change the session name
Step 5: Submit the form
Step 6: Verify the name is updated

description: Trainer can set, change, and remove session password
steps: Step 1: Log in as a trainer
Step 2: Generate a training session with no password
Step 3: Navigate to session edit page
Step 4: Set a password and submit
Step 5: Verify password_digest is stored (bcrypt-hashed)
Step 6: Change the password to a new value
Step 7: Verify the new password works
Step 8: Remove the password (make session open-access)
Step 9: Verify session is accessible without a password

description: Trainer can delete a training session without affecting master training
steps: Step 1: Log in as a trainer
Step 2: Generate a training session from a master training
Step 3: Delete the training session
Step 4: Verify confirmation dialog is shown before deletion
Step 5: Verify the session is deleted
Step 6: Verify the master training and other sessions are unaffected

description: Public session view renders snapshot content without authentication
steps: Step 1: Generate a training session without a password
Step 2: Navigate to /s/:slug as an unauthenticated user
Step 3: Verify the session content is rendered without login
Step 4: Verify slides are listed with download buttons
Step 5: Verify prerequisite assets are listed with download buttons
Step 6: Verify exercises are rendered as styled HTML navigable by tab or sidebar

description: Password-protected session requires password gate before showing content
steps: Step 1: Generate a training session with a password set
Step 2: Navigate to /s/:slug as an unauthenticated user
Step 3: Verify redirect to /s/:slug/unlock (password gate form)
Step 4: Enter an incorrect password
Step 5: Verify access is denied with an error message
Step 6: Enter the correct password
Step 7: Verify a signed httpOnly cookie is set with 24-hour expiry
Step 8: Verify redirect to /s/:slug with full content rendered
Step 9: Refresh the page and verify content is still accessible (cookie persists)

description: Session view download buttons serve files via expiring signed URLs
steps: Step 1: Generate a session with slides and prerequisite assets
Step 2: Access the public session view
Step 3: Click a download button for a slide
Step 4: Verify the request hits /training_sessions/:id/download/:attachment_id
Step 5: Verify the response redirects to a signed GCS URL with 15-minute expiry

description: Session view exercises are navigable by sidebar
steps: Step 1: Generate a session with three exercises
Step 2: Access the public session view
Step 3: Navigate to the Exercises tab
Step 4: Verify a sidebar lists all exercises by title
Step 5: Click on different exercises in the sidebar
Step 6: Verify the main panel renders the selected exercise's body_html as sanitized HTML

description: Session view is read-only with no editing capabilities
steps: Step 1: Access a public session view
Step 2: Verify no edit buttons or forms are present
Step 3: Verify content cannot be modified from this view

description: Complete end-to-end trainer workflow: create training, add content, generate session, share
steps: Step 1: Log in as a trainer
Step 2: Create a new master training with title and description
Step 3: Upload two slide files
Step 4: Upload a prerequisite asset with description
Step 5: Add two exercises with rich text content
Step 6: Reorder the exercises
Step 7: Generate a training session
Step 8: Set a password for the session
Step 9: Copy the share link
Step 10: Open the share link in a new browser (unauthenticated)
Step 11: Enter the session password
Step 12: Verify all content (slides, assets, exercises) is displayed correctly
Step 13: Download a slide and verify it works

description: Complete end-to-end super admin workflow: create account, add admin, verify access
steps: Step 1: Log in as super admin
Step 2: Create a new client account with initial admin email and password
Step 3: Verify the account appears in the clients list
Step 4: Log out
Step 5: Log in as the new account admin
Step 6: Verify the admin can access account settings
Step 7: Verify the admin can view the trainer roster
Step 8: Invite a trainer
Step 9: Log out and log in as super admin
Step 10: Delete the client account
Step 11: Verify all associated data is cascade-deleted

description: Trainer scoping: trainers can only see their own account's trainings
steps: Step 1: Create two client accounts, each with a trainer
Step 2: Trainer A creates a master training
Step 3: Log in as Trainer B
Step 4: Navigate to /master_trainings
Step 5: Verify Trainer A's training is not visible to Trainer B

description: Active Storage direct upload with progress indicator
steps: Step 1: Log in as a trainer
Step 2: Navigate to a master training detail page
Step 3: Start uploading a large file
Step 4: Verify a progress bar is displayed during upload
Step 5: Verify the file appears in the list after upload completes

description: Trix editor supports headings, bold, italic, lists, links, and image attachments
steps: Step 1: Log in as a trainer
Step 2: Navigate to create a new exercise
Step 3: Add a heading in the Trix editor
Step 4: Add bold and italic text
Step 5: Add a bullet list and a numbered list
Step 6: Add a hyperlink
Step 7: Upload an image attachment
Step 8: Submit the form
Step 9: Verify all formatting is preserved when viewing the exercise

description: User status enum works correctly: active, inactive, pending_password_change
steps: Step 1: Create a user with status active
Step 2: Verify the user can log in
Step 3: Change status to inactive
Step 4: Verify the user cannot log in
Step 5: Change status to pending_password_change
Step 6: Log in and verify redirect to change-password page

description: Session unlock rate limiting blocks excessive password attempts
steps: Step 1: Create a password-protected training session
Step 2: Navigate to /s/:slug/unlock
Step 3: Submit 11 incorrect password attempts rapidly
Step 4: Verify requests are throttled via Rack::Attack (10 req/min per IP)

description: Session passwords are bcrypt-hashed and never exposed in views or logs
steps: Step 1: Set a password on a training session
Step 2: Verify password_digest is stored as a bcrypt hash in the database
Step 3: Navigate to the session edit page
Step 4: Verify the plaintext password is not visible in the form or page source

description: All destructive actions show confirmation dialogs
steps: Step 1: Attempt to delete a client account
Step 2: Verify a confirmation dialog appears before proceeding
Step 3: Attempt to delete a master training
Step 4: Verify a confirmation dialog appears
Step 5: Attempt to delete a training session
Step 6: Verify a confirmation dialog appears
Step 7: Attempt to remove a trainer permanently
Step 8: Verify a confirmation modal appears

description: Form validation shows errors with red field borders and inline messages
steps: Step 1: Navigate to the login form
Step 2: Submit with empty fields
Step 3: Verify validation errors are displayed with red field borders and inline messages
Step 4: Navigate to create a master training
Step 5: Submit with empty title
Step 6: Verify validation error is shown

description: Empty state partials displayed when no data exists
steps: Step 1: Log in as super admin
Step 2: Navigate to /admin/clients with no client accounts
Step 3: Verify an empty state message or partial is displayed
Step 4: Log in as a trainer with no master trainings
Step 5: Navigate to /master_trainings
Step 6: Verify an empty state message is displayed

description: Flash notifications appear as slide-in toasts and auto-dismiss
steps: Step 1: Perform an action that triggers a flash notice (e.g., create a training)
Step 2: Verify a slide-in toast notification appears
Step 3: Wait 4 seconds
Step 4: Verify the notification auto-dismisses

description: Copy-to-clipboard works for session share links
steps: Step 1: Log in as a trainer
Step 2: Navigate to the session list page
Step 3: Click the copy share link button for a session
Step 4: Verify the session URL is copied to the clipboard
Step 5: Verify visual feedback is shown (e.g., button text changes)

description: Login page is styled with Tailwind CSS
steps: Step 1: Navigate to /session/new
Step 2: Verify the login form has Tailwind-styled inputs, button, and layout
Step 3: Verify the page is visually polished with proper spacing, colors, and typography

description: Admin dashboard has a clean, professional layout
steps: Step 1: Log in as super admin
Step 2: Navigate to /admin
Step 3: Verify the admin home page has a well-structured layout
Step 4: Verify navigation and signout are clearly visible

description: Client accounts list is displayed as a styled table
steps: Step 1: Log in as super admin
Step 2: Navigate to /admin/clients with existing accounts
Step 3: Verify accounts are displayed in a clean table with columns for name, creation date, and trainer count
Step 4: Verify delete buttons are styled as destructive actions

description: Trainer roster has role badges and status pills
steps: Step 1: Log in as account admin
Step 2: Navigate to /account/trainers
Step 3: Verify each trainer has a colored status pill (active=green, inactive=red, pending=yellow)
Step 4: Verify role badges are displayed alongside user names

description: Master training dashboard shows card grid layout
steps: Step 1: Log in as a trainer
Step 2: Navigate to /master_trainings with multiple trainings
Step 3: Verify trainings are displayed as cards in a grid layout
Step 4: Verify each card shows title, description, session count, and timestamps

description: Master training detail page has tabbed layout for slides, assets, and exercises
steps: Step 1: Log in as a trainer
Step 2: Navigate to a master training detail page
Step 3: Verify the page has tabs or sections for Slides, Prerequisite Assets, and Exercises
Step 4: Verify each section has clear upload controls and list displays

description: Trix editor is styled with Tailwind prose classes
steps: Step 1: Log in as a trainer
Step 2: Navigate to the exercise editor
Step 3: Verify the Trix editor toolbar is well-styled
Step 4: Verify the content area uses Tailwind prose classes for readable typography

description: Public session view uses a clean layout with no navigation bar
steps: Step 1: Access a public session view at /s/:slug
Step 2: Verify the page has a minimal, clean layout with no main navigation bar
Step 3: Verify content is well-organized with clear sections for slides, assets, and exercises

description: Public session view has tabbed layout with Stimulus tab controller
steps: Step 1: Access a public session view
Step 2: Verify tabs for Slides, Prerequisites, and Exercises are displayed
Step 3: Click between tabs
Step 4: Verify content switches smoothly without full page reload

description: Password gate page is styled and user-friendly
steps: Step 1: Navigate to /s/:slug/unlock for a password-protected session
Step 2: Verify the password form is centered and well-styled
Step 3: Verify error messages for incorrect passwords are clearly displayed

description: File upload has progress bar and clear feedback
steps: Step 1: Log in as a trainer
Step 2: Start uploading a file via direct upload
Step 3: Verify a progress bar is visible during upload
Step 4: Verify success feedback is displayed after upload completes
Step 5: Verify error feedback is displayed if upload fails

description: Drag-and-drop reordering has smooth visual feedback
steps: Step 1: Log in as a trainer
Step 2: Navigate to a master training with multiple slides
Step 3: Start dragging a slide
Step 4: Verify visual feedback (e.g., ghost element, highlight) during drag
Step 5: Drop the slide in a new position
Step 6: Verify the list updates smoothly

description: Responsive layout: all pages work on mobile viewports
steps: Step 1: Open the application on a mobile viewport (375px width)
Step 2: Navigate through login, admin, trainer dashboard, and public session view
Step 3: Verify sidebars collapse appropriately
Step 4: Verify tables scroll horizontally on small screens
Step 5: Verify touch targets are large enough for mobile interaction

description: Accessible markup: ARIA roles, labels, and focus traps
steps: Step 1: Navigate through key pages with a screen reader or inspect HTML
Step 2: Verify modals have ARIA roles and focus traps
Step 3: Verify all form inputs have associated labels
Step 4: Verify interactive elements are keyboard-navigable

description: Confirmation modals are styled with clear destructive action indicators
steps: Step 1: Trigger a delete action (e.g., delete training session)
Step 2: Verify the confirmation modal has a clear warning message
Step 3: Verify the confirm button is styled as a destructive action (e.g., red)
Step 4: Verify the cancel button is clearly distinguishable

description: Flash toast notifications are visually appealing slide-in toasts
steps: Step 1: Trigger a success flash (e.g., create a resource)
Step 2: Verify toast slides in from the top or corner
Step 3: Verify toast has appropriate color (green for success, red for error)
Step 4: Verify toast auto-dismisses after 4 seconds with a fade animation

description: Password reset and forgot password pages are styled consistently
steps: Step 1: Navigate to /passwords/new
Step 2: Verify the forgot password form is styled consistently with the login page
Step 3: Navigate to /passwords/:token/edit
Step 4: Verify the reset password form matches the overall design system
